what_it_does: Renders a two-phone design mockup for a mobile app focus screen where a piece is reviewed full-screen with dials, pins, navigation rail icons, and a secondary opened-pin review state.
observations: The document is a standalone HTML mockup with embedded CSS and a small inline script.
It imports Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts, plus shared piece and phone stylesheets.
The left phone shows a large mini piece preview with title text, three vertical color plates, hatch texture, two numbered pin markers, and two dial rows.
The right phone shows an opened pin view labeled PIN 2 with a quoted prompt, a small state inset, a ghost action, and a keep action.
The script fills the bottom rail and chat marker using DNA.org.icon from /surface/runtime/organisms.js.
Most layout in the file is absolute-positioned and uses design tokens such as --dna-space-* and --dna-type-*.
The visible framing text positions this as a focus/review screen with dials and pin-based review travel.
standouts: The mockup pairs a whole-piece inspection state with a focused opened-pin state in the same composition.
Numeric and symbolic review controls are represented together: warmth has a value, density has a placeholder dot, and pins attach comments to the piece.
The design uses miniature artifact rendering inside a phone frame rather than only listing app controls.
conventions: Uses shared global phone/piece classes while defining file-local CSS for the specific composition.
Uses CSS custom properties for spacing, radius, color, and type scale.
Uses inline style attributes for per-element color and pin placement.
Uses a runtime icon helper instead of hardcoded SVG markup for rail and chat icons.
Keeps the mockup static except for client-side icon insertion.
concerns: Several key positions and dimensions are fixed in pixels, which makes the mockup tightly bound to the current phone frame.
The density dial shows a dot instead of a numeric value, and the file does not explain whether that is intentional state or placeholder content.
The inline script assumes DNA.org.icon and specific icon names are available, with no local fallback.
The actionable-looking controls are static divs without semantic buttons or interaction handlers.
notes: This file is a static mobile design specimen showing a piece-review focus screen with pins, dials, and an opened annotation state.
questions: Is the density value intentionally unavailable, or is the dot placeholder copy?
Are the pins meant to be interactive in a later layer, or only visual markers in this specimen?
Should the rail icon selection indicate the compass/focus section specifically, or just the current mockup state?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated focus-screen purpose matches the actual content: the file presents a full-screen piece review view with nearby dials and a related opened-pin review view.
intention_signals: It seems intended to communicate how a mobile app can let someone inspect a design piece, adjust or read evaluative dials, and open pinned notes without leaving the review context.
novelty: The file treats the reviewed artifact itself as the center of the phone UI and overlays review pins and calibration dials around it, rather than presenting the review as a separate form.
contribution: Contributes a mobile focus-screen substrate with artifact-centered review, pinned annotations, compact dial controls, bottom navigation icons, and a paired opened-note state that a fused system would keep as a representation of in-context critique.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone HTML/CSS mockup of a two-phone mobile focus screen for reviewing a design piece with pins, dials, rail icons, and an opened annotation view.
intention_for_embedding: This was for expressing an app review mode where the work remains visible while critique, calibration, and note inspection happen around it.
